description: Phase-3 PvP back-test first cut (2026-07-04). Two-sided engine (pvp_engine.py) with round-4 BEST_PARAMS + one rate knob, run over the 8 validated PvP reports (pvp_backtest.py). Symmetric-assumption baseline BEFORE the attacker/defender asymmetry fix.

status.stat_skill_fix: DONE - hero stat skills now multiplicative (assemble.py board.skillmult); QA finding closed.
status.engine_plumbing: DONE - assembly + two-sided sim + harness run all 8 reports without error.
status.rate_scale: rate~70 gives realistic ~30-turn durations (rate=1 gave 2000-4000 turns).
status.accuracy: POOR - systematic defender bias; winner correct only 2/8; casualty magnitudes off, esp. defender under-killed.

backtest_rate70.note: worst miss - defender barely scratched, actually got wiped

diagnosis: The two-sided engine assumed SYMMETRIC combat (both sides fire all units linearly). The farm mechanism (rounds 1-4) was ASYMMETRIC: attacker deals FULL linear damage to the compact target; the defender/beast returns fire FRONTAGE-LIMITED (~N_attacker^ed, sublinear). Symmetric assumption over-powers the defender (fortress walls at full output chip the attacker too hard). The systematic defender-win bias is the signature.
next: Restructure pvp_engine defender output to mirror the farm beast->own term (attacker-linear / defender-frontage-limited), then calibrate the PvP coupling (rate + asymmetry) on the r6/r8 SISTER PAIR only and validate on the other 6 - do NOT fit all 8 (overfitting). Only after that: proc scheduler for stochastic runs.
caution: 8 reports is a THIN validation set for a multi-param engine. Calibrate on 2 (r6/r8), hold out 6. Report win/loss accuracy and casualty-magnitude bands, not point fits.
